paper_title: Means to Study Ca 2+ and Actin Signaling

methods.description: Calcium imaging is a technique used to study the change of cytosolic calcium concentration ([Ca 2+ ] cyt) in living plant cells. It helps in understanding the complex patterns of calcium concentration changes in response to various stimuli.

steps.description: Use Ca 2+ sensing fluorescent dyes (e.g., Fluo-3, Fura-2) to detect [Ca 2+ ] cyt changes in plant cells.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Ca 2+ sensing fluorescent dyes to plant cells

steps.description: Use FRET-based Ca 2+ sensors, such as yellow cameleon (YC) 2.1 and YC 3.6, for more sensitive detection of [Ca 2+ ] cyt changes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply FRET-based Ca 2+ sensors to plant cells

steps.description: Use a fluorescent microscope for Ca 2+ sensing fluorescent dye application and a confocal microscope for FRET-based sensor methods.
steps.searchable_summary: use microscopes to visualize calcium changes

methods.method_name: Patch Clamp Technique for Ca 2+ Channels
methods.description: Patch clamp technique is used to record the activity of Ca 2+ channels in plant cells, which are crucial for understanding the mechanisms of Ca 2+ influx and cytosolic calcium rise.

steps.description: Record Ca 2+ channels in plant cells using the patch clamp technique.
steps.searchable_summary: apply patch clamp technique to record Ca 2+ channels

steps.description: Analyze the activation and gating mechanisms of various Ca 2+ -permeable channels.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze activation and gating of Ca 2+ channels

methods.description: Actin imaging techniques are used to study the function and organization of actin during plant development and signaling, using both pharmacological agents and fluorescent methods.

steps.description: Use pharmacological agents to disrupt, stabilize, or block actin filaments.
steps.searchable_summary: apply pharmacological agents to modify actin filaments

steps.description: Use fluorescent proteins fused with actin binding domains to visualize actin organization and dynamics.
steps.searchable_summary: use fluorescent proteins to visualize actin

steps.description: Use a green to red photo-convertible probe, mEosFp::FABD-mTn, for precise labeling of F-actin.
steps.searchable_summary: apply photo-convertible probe for F-actin labeling
